domain: Korean ondol underfloor heating (agungi furnace + horizontal gorae stone-floor flue + flat gudeul-jang stones; single-source horizontal cascade with progressive stone-mediated heat absorption; intentional temperature gradient hot-near-furnace cooler-near-chimney)
specific_mechanism: Single-source horizontal heat cascade with stone-mediated progressive absorption producing stable spatial temperature gradient; thermal mass + delayed re-radiation across path.
llm_application: ONDOL-CASCADE: a learning-rate-injection schedule where the warmup boost is applied at ONE specific layer ℓ₀ (e.g., layer 0 — the 'furnace') and CASCADES laterally through neighbouring layers with thermal-mass decay α^|ℓ−ℓ₀| (α∈(0,1) per-layer absorption). Effective LR at layer ℓ = global_lr × (1 + boost × α^|ℓ−ℓ₀| × t_warmup_active). The boost is TEMPORALLY SCHEDULED (active during warmup t<T_warmup) AND SPATIALLY DECAYED (α^distance). Combined with a per-layer thermal-mass DELAY: layer ℓ feels the boost with delay τ × |ℓ−ℓ₀|. Differs from LLRD (static layer-wise exponential without injection point) by being SINGLE-POINT TIME-AND-SPACE EXPLICIT.
novelty_claim: LLRD applies static exponential LR decay across all layers from training start. WSD uses warmup-stable-decay as a single global schedule. Progressive Residual Warmup (2603.05369) coordinates layer-wise learning across warmup/stable/decay phases but uses a residual-component reparametrization not lateral cascade. ONDOL-CASCADE differs by (a) SINGLE-POINT INJECTION at one layer ℓ₀, (b) LATERAL SPATIAL DECAY α^|ℓ−ℓ₀|, (c) PER-LAYER DELAY τ × |ℓ−ℓ₀|, (d) THERMAL-MASS interpretation governing each layer's effective absorption coefficient.
